stem: Coal production in a certain region dropped from 6 million tons in 2005 to 2.4 million tons in 2017. Assuming production decreased at a constant rate, which of the following linear functions f best models the production, in millions of tons, t years after 2005?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The slope is (2.4 - 6)/(17 - 5) = -3.6/12 = -0.30 million tons per year. At t = 0 (year 2005), production is 6, so f(t) = -0.30t + 6. Verification at t = 12: f(12) = -3.6 + 6 = 2.4 ✓.

Choice A has slope and intercept swapped. Choice C uses a positive slope instead of negative. Choice D uses the wrong y-intercept.
